jobs.job_title: Data Analyst - SQL Anywhere in India/Multiple Locations/Mumbai/Bangalore
jobs.job_description: Company Overview : Reneassiance Academy of Wisdom is an emerging leader in the EdTech sector, dedicated to transforming professional development through data-driven learning experiences. The company operates a comprehensive digital platform that bridges the gap between academic theory and industry-ready skills for learners across India. By leveraging advanced analytics to personalize educational pathways, the academy empowers thousands of students to achieve career milestones in competitive professional landscapes.Role Overview : As a Data Analyst, you will serve as the bridge between raw educational data and actionable business intelligence. You will work closely with product managers, content strategists, and leadership teams to interpret user behavior, track learning outcomes, and optimize platform performance. Your insights will directly influence how the academy designs its curriculum and engages its user base, ensuring that every decision is backed by rigorous quantitative evidence to drive student success and business growth.Key Responsibilities : - Analyze large datasets from the learning platform to identify trends in student engagement and course completion rates, enabling the product team to refine content delivery.- Build and maintain automated dashboards that provide stakeholders with real-time visibility into key performance indicators and operational metrics.- Conduct deep-dive investigations into user drop-off points to recommend specific UI/UX improvements that enhance the overall learning journey.- Collaborate with the marketing team to evaluate the effectiveness of outreach campaigns, ensuring resources are allocated toward the highest-converting channels.- Translate complex technical findings into clear, non-technical reports for senior management to support strategic decision-making processes.Required Skillset : - Proficiency in SQL for complex data extraction and manipulation, combined with advanced Excel skills for financial and operational modeling.- Demonstrated ability to visualize data using tools like Tableau or Power BI to tell a compelling story that drives organizational action.- Strong statistical aptitude to perform cohort analysis, A/B testing, and predictive modeling to forecast student retention.- Excellent interpersonal skills to effectively communicate technical insights to cross-functional teams and influence stakeholders at various levels.- A Bachelors degree in Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, or a related quantitative field from a premier institute.- High degree of adaptability to thrive in a distributed work environment, with the ability to manage projects independently across Mumbai, Bangalore, and remote locations. (ref:hirist.tech)

Job Title:

Business Analyst / Data Analyst / Marketing Analyst

Job Type:

Full-time

Job Summary

We are looking for a skilled and motivated Data Analyst with expertise in SAS, Python, SQL, and Tableau to support data-driven marketing analytics and strategy initiatives in the BFSI (Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance) domain. The ideal candidate will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to develop insightful dashboards and scalable SAS processes for multiple clients.

Key Responsibilities

📌 Marketing Campaign Strategy & Audience Development
• Design data-driven marketing strategies for banking products based on historical campaign performance.
• Develop AI/ML models to optimize campaign ROI and customer targeting.
• Build and validate campaign audiences aligned with approved strategies.

📊 Campaign Performance Analysis & Reporting
• Enhance and maintain Tableau dashboards with a focus on usability and visual storytelling for non-technical stakeholders.
• Conduct in-depth data analysis using SAS to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
• Perform segmentation using decision trees and clustering techniques.
• Execute statistical A/B testing to evaluate campaign effectiveness.
• Translate client requirements into technical specifications and deliver data-backed solutions.
• Prepare and present findings through impactful PowerPoint presentations and ad hoc reports.

Required Skills & Qualifications
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, or a related field.
• Minimum 3 years of experience in analytics, preferably in marketing or BFSI.
• Proficient in SAS, SQL, and/or Python for data manipulation and analysis.
• At least 1 year of experience in building dashboards using Tableau or Power BI.
• Strong analytical thinking, problem-solving, and data storytelling skills.
• Excellent communication and interpersonal abilities.
• Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced environment.
• Experience in the banking or financial services sector is a strong plus.
• Certifications in SAS or Tableau are advantageous.

Additional Requirements
• Must be available to work evening shifts to support global clients.
• Work from office: Candidates must be willing to relocate to Gurugram or Ahmedabad.

Senior Data Analyst - Data Platform

Discipline: Data Analytics

Location: Gurugram)

Shift Time - 12 PM to 8:30 PM IST

Role Summary

As a Senior Data Analyst, you will play a critical role in supporting Asia data modernization, IBOR/ABOR initiatives, and enterprise analytics. You will partner closely with Investment, Finance, Risk, and Technology teams to analyze complex datasets, define data requirements, and translate business needs into well‑governed, analytics‑ready data products.

This is a senior, business‑facing analytics role, requiring strong domain knowledge in asset management, a solid understanding of investment data (transactions, positions, valuations), and the ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders. You will help replicate and extend key skill sets currently anchored in the team, enabling continuity and scale for Asia‑focused initiatives.

Your objective is to improve decision‑making, data transparency, and operational efficiency by ensuring high‑quality, reconciled, and well‑understood data is available for reporting, analytics, and regulatory use cases.

Key Accountabilities

Investment & Data Analysis

Analyze and interpret complex investment and accounting datasets (transactions, positions, cash, valuations) across asset classes.

Support Asia IBOR and ABOR initiatives, including data reconciliation, validation, and readiness for downstream reporting.

Partner with investment stakeholders to understand workflows, pain points, and analytical needs.

Data Requirements & Governance

Define and document data requirements, business rules, and analytical logic for enterprise data products.

Contribute to data governance artifacts, including data dictionaries, lineage, and quality checks.

Work closely with Business Team, Data Platform and Engineering teams to ensure analytical requirements are accurately implemented.

Cross‑Functional Collaboration

Act as a bridge between business users and technical teams, translating analytical needs into clear, actionable inputs.

Collaborate with Data Engineers, BI developers, and Product Owners to support self‑serve analytics and standardized reporting.

Participate in requirements elicitation, backlog refinement, and solution reviews.

Reporting & Insights

Support and validate datasets used in Power BI and enterprise reporting.

Perform root‑cause analysis, reconciliations, and exception reviews to ensure data accuracy and completeness.

Enable power users with clear, trusted data for decision‑making.

Competencies / Qualifications

8+ years of experience in data analytics within asset management, investment operations, or financial services.

Strong understanding of investment data domains including:

Trades / Transactions

Positions & holdings

Valuations, cash flows, and accounting data (IBOR / ABOR concepts)

CFA designation preferred, or currently pursuing CFA.

Experience working with front‑office, middle‑office, or finance stakeholders.

Strong Excel, SQL and Python skills and experience analyzing data in Snowflake or similar data platforms.

Familiarity with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.

Experience with data reconciliation, data quality checks, and governance frameworks.

Exposure to AI/LLM‑based data solutions is a plus.

Prior experience with Big4 or similar consulting background is a strong advantage.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to non‑technical stakeholders.

Bachelor’s or master’s degree in Finance, Engineering, Computer Science, or a related field.

Requirements
Competencies / Qualifications

8+ years of experience in data analytics within asset management, investment operations, or financial services.

Strong understanding of investment data domains including:

Trades / Transactions

Positions & holdings

Valuations, cash flows, and accounting data (IBOR / ABOR concepts)

CFA designation preferred, or currently pursuing CFA.

Experience working with front‑office, middle‑office, or finance stakeholders.

Strong Excel, SQL and Python skills and experience analyzing data in Snowflake or similar data platforms.

Familiarity with Power BI, Tableau, or similar BI tools.

Experience with data reconciliation, data quality checks, and governance frameworks.

Exposure to AI/LLM‑based data solutions is a plus.

Prior experience with Big4 or similar consulting background is a strong advantage.

Excellent communication skills with the ability to explain complex data concepts to non‑technical stakeholders.

Bachelor’s or master’s degre

Company Description

About Sopra Steria

Sopra Steria, a major Tech player in Europe with 51,000 employees in nearly 30 countries, is recognised for its consulting, digital services and solutions. It helps its clients drive their digital transformation and obtain tangible and sustainable benefits. The Group provides end-to-end solutions to make large companies and organisations more competitive by combining in-depth knowledge of a wide range of business sectors and innovative technologies with a collaborative approach. Sopra Steria places people at the heart of everything it does and is committed to putting digital to work for its clients in order to build a positive future for all. In 2025, the Group generated revenues of €5.6 billion.

The world is how we shape it.

Job Description

Key Responsibilities:
• Design, develop, and maintain workflows using Alteryx Designer and Alteryx Server
• Build automated ETL/data transformation pipelines for enterprise reporting and analytics.
• Integrate data from multiple sources including databases, cloud platforms, APIs, Excel, ERP, and CRM systems.
• Optimize existing Alteryx workflows for performance, scalability, and maintainability.
• Collaborate with business users, data analysts, and BI teams to gather requirements and deliver analytics solutions.
• Develop reusable workflow components, macros, and data quality frameworks.
• Perform data cleansing, validation, reconciliation, and transformation activities.
• Support reporting and visualization platforms such as Oracle BI ,**Power BI** at times .
• Troubleshoot production issues and provide timely resolutions.
• Mentor junior developers and support team capability building.
• Work closely with Data Engineering and Data Warehouse teams to support enterprise data initiatives.

Primary Skills:
• Strong hands-on experience in **Alteryx Designer**
• Experience with **Alteryx Server / Gallery**
• Workflow optimization and automation expertise
• Strong ETL and Data Integration knowledge
• Data preparation and transformation expertise

Alteryx certifications preferred:

Total Experience Expected: 06-08 years

Qualifications

BTECH/MCA

Additional Information

Open to Work in UK Shift

At our organization, we are committed to fighting against all forms of discrimination. We foster a work environment that is inclusive and respectful of all differences.

All of our positions are open to people with disabilities.

We are looking for a Data Scientist / Data Science Intern to join our team at at Nettech India, contributing to software, data, or IT system development and support. This role offers an in-hand salary of ₹25000 - ₹40000 and a chance to work in a fast-paced, technology-driven environment.
Key Responsibilities:
• Develop, test, or maintain software applications, databases, or analytical tools

• Analyze data, generate insights, and ensure system accuracy

• Troubleshoot issues and support daily IT operations

• Collaborate with teams to deliver high-quality, scalable solutions

• Follow documentation, security, and testing standards

Job Requirements:
The minimum qualification for this role is Graduate and 1 - 4 years of experience. Hands-on knowledge of SQL, Python, MySQL, Excel / Advanced Excel, Power BI / Tableau, Problem Solving, Communication and modern tools is essential. Strong analytical, problem-solving, and communication skills required.

• It is a Full Time IT / Software / Data Analyst job for candidates with 1 - 4 years of experience.

This role is for one of Weekday's clients

Min Experience: 3+ years

Location: Nagpur

JobType: full-time

We are seeking a Senior Data Analyst with strong SQL, Python, and business analytics skills to work across diverse business domains including supply chain, education, and energy management. This role demands a professional who can quickly grasp new business contexts, apply rigorous analytical thinking, and translate data insights into tangible financial and operational impact.

Requirements

Key Responsibilities

• Solve advanced SQL problem statements to extract, transform, and analyze large, complex datasets across varied domains
• Build models and dashboards supporting decision-making in supply chain, education, and/or energy management projects
• Analyze the financial impact of inventory decisions including holding costs, stockout costs, working capital, and cost-benefit of allocation/MOQ strategies
• Calculate and refine Minimum Order Quantities (MOQ) based on demand, lead times, and cost trade-offs
• Design inventory allocation strategies balancing service levels, cost, and financial efficiency
• Analyze new customer acquisition trends, funnel performance, and growth metrics
• Translate business problems into structured analytical frameworks and present findings with clear financial/business implications to leadership
• Partner with cross-functional and cross-project teams, adapting analytical approach to each domain's context
• Write efficient Python scripts for automation, data pipelines, and analysis

Qualifications

• 3-5 years of experience in data analytics; supply chain/inventory analytics background preferred
• Advanced SQL proficiency (complex joins, window functions, CTEs, query optimization)
• Strong Python skills for data analysis (Pandas, NumPy)
• Experience translating operational data (e.g., inventory, resource usage) into financial impact analysis (cost, ROI, working capital, efficiency metrics)
• High adaptability - proven ability to work across different business domains/projects
• Strong business and financial acumen - able to connect data insights to P&L or cost outcomes
• Excellent communication skills for presenting to varied stakeholder groups across projects
• Experience with BI tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker, or similar)

Required Skills

SQL | Python | Business Analytics | Supply Chain Analytics | Inventory Analytics | Data Modeling | Dashboarding | Financial Analysis | BI Tools | Adaptability | Business Acumen | Communication | Data Engineering | Cloud Platforms |Statistical Modeling

Benefits & Perks

• Direct ownership and close collaboration with the founder

Must-have Skills

Data Analytics, Tableau, SQL

Good-to-have Skills

Python, Excel, Power BI

Orange Business is here!

About us

Join us at Orange Business!

We are a network and digital integrator that understands the entire value chain of the digital world, freeing our customers to focus on the strategic initiatives that shape their business.

Every day, you will collaborate with a team dedicated to providing consistent, sustainable global solutions, no matter where our customers operate. With over 30,000 employees across Asia, the Americas, Africa, and Europe, we offer a dynamic environment to develop and perfect your skills in a field filled with exciting challenges and opportunities.
About the role

We are seeking a skilled Business Analyst with expertise in AI, data automation, and Power BI with AI capabilities. This role focuses on leveraging advanced analytics and AI-powered tools to transform raw data into actionable insights, driving business growth and operational efficiency.

About you

Mission
• The mission of the Business Data Analyst role is to leverage data-driven insights for optimizing business processes and enhancing operational efficiency. Key objectives include supporting 5strategic initiatives through advanced data analytics, fostering a culture of data-driven decision-making, and driving growth through actionable insights.

Main activities
• Analyze and interpret complex datasets using machine learning libraries or AI-enabled analytics platforms to uncover trends, patterns, and actionable insights.
• Automate data processes, including extraction, transformation, and loading (ETL), using AI-driven automation tools to improve efficiency.
• Develop predictive models and perform advanced analytics to support forecasting, customer segmentation, and other business needs.
• Design, develop, and maintain interactive Power BI dashboards and reports enhanced with AI functionalities for business decision-making.
• Ensure data accuracy and integrity by validating, cleaning, and preprocessing datasets.
• Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify business challenges and propose data-driven solutions.
• Stay updated with the latest advancements in AI, automation, and Power BI to continuously enhance analytical capabilities.

Experience Range

4-5 years of experience into Business Analyst role and 1+ years of working into AI capabilities.

You bring rigor, passion for challenges, and determination. You seek the opportunity to expand your expertise, achieve your goals, and thrive.

What we offer

• Global Opportunities: Work in multi-national teams with opportunity to collaborate with colleagues and customers from all over the world.
• Flexible Work Environment: Flexible working hours and possibility to combine work from office and home (hybrid ways of working).
• Professional Development: training programs and upskilling/re-skilling opportunities.
• Career Growth: Internal growth and mobility opportunities within Orange.
• Caring and Daring Culture: Health and well-being programs and benefits, diversity & inclusion initiatives, CSR and employee connect events.
• Reward Programs: Employee Referral Program, Change Maker Awards.

Only your skills matterRegardless of your age, gender identity, race, ethnic origin, religion/belief, sexual orientation, marital status, neuroatypia, disability, veteran status or appearance, we encourage diversity within our teams because it is a strength for the collective and a vector of innovation. Orange Group is a disabled-friendly company and equal opportunity employer: don't hesitate to tell us about your specific needs.

At Orange, only your skills matter.

Regardless of your age, gender, background, origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, neurodiversity, or appearance, we actively encourage diversity within our teams, as it is a collective strength and a driver of innovation.Orange is a disability-inclusive employer: please feel free to let us know about any specific needs you may have.

About Northern Trust: 

Northern Trust, a Fortune 500 company, is a globally recognized, award-winning financial institution that has been in continuous operation since 1889. 

Northern Trust is proud to provide innovative financial services and guidance to the world's most successful individuals, families, and institutions by remaining true to our enduring principles of service, expertise, and integrity. With more than 130 years of financial experience and over 22,000 partners, we serve the world's most sophisticated clients using leading technology and exceptional service. 

Job Description 

Position Overview: We are seeking a hands-on Senior Data Analyst to join our asset management team. This role requires a dynamic professional with at least 3 years of experience who can serve as the data subject matter expert (SME) within a cross-functional Scrum team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for driving complex data analysis projects, supporting data mapping, validation and analytics. 

Key Responsibilities: 
• Lead data analysis and reporting initiatives to deliver actionable insights for asset management operations. 
• Drive sourcing of new datasets, functional design and mapping, data modeling and proactive identification and mitigation of data validation and quality issues. 
• Demonstrate advanced competence in database systems, including SQL and Power BI. 
• Apply strong mathematical, analytical, and problem-solving skills to address business challenges. 
• Act as the primary data SME within a Scrum team, collaborating with stakeholders and facilitating discussions to reach decisions. 
• Build and maintain strong relationships within the team and across departments. 
• Deliver high-quality work products requiring minimal revision and ensure significant financial, client, and internal business impact. 
• Provide mentorship and guidance to less experienced team members, fostering knowledge sharing and professional development. 
• Participate in business stakeholder meetings and interact with Senior Management, presenting data-driven recommendations. Technical Requirements: 
• Proficiency in Asset Management data including AUM & Flows, Performance & Attribution, Fees, Benchmark and Securities, Account onboarding, ESG etc. 
• Proficiency in relational database concepts, querying, and data warehouse management. 
• Hands-on experience with analytics tools such as Microsoft Business Intelligence Suite, QlikView, or similar. 
• Ability to manipulate and analyze large datasets, translating findings into clear, actionable insights. 
• Experience in requirements gathering and using decision support tools. Qualifications: 
• For internal applications - 24 months as Analyst(B4) 
• Bachelor's degree in a related field (e.g., Economics, Finance, Mathematics, Computer Science) or equivalent work experience. 
• Minimum of 3 years' hands-on experience in data analysis, preferably within asset management or financial services. 
• Strong communication and project management skills. 
• Demonstrated ability to work effectively in a Scrum/agile environment. Working with Us: 

As a Northern Trust partner, greater achievements await. You will be part of a flexible and collaborative work culture in an organization where financial strength and stability is an asset that emboldens us to explore new ideas. 

Movement within the organization is encouraged, senior leaders are accessible, and you can take pride in working for a company committed to assisting the communities we serve! Join a workplace with a greater purpose. 

We'd love to learn more about how your interests and experience could be a fit with one of the world's most admired and sustainable companies! Build your career with us and apply today. #MadeForGreater 

Reasonable accommodation 

Northern Trust is committed to working with and providing reasonable accommodations to individuals with disabilities. If you need a reasonable accommodation for any part of the employment process, please email our HR Service Center at MyHRHelp@ntrs.com . 

We hope you're excited about the role and the opportunity to work with us. We value an inclusive workplace and understand flexibility means different things to different people. 

Apply today and talk to us about your flexible working requirements and together we can achieve greater. 

About Our Bangalore Office 

The Northern Trust Bangalore office, established in 2005, is home to over 5,600 employees. In this stunning office, space, we offer fantastic amenities which include our Arrival Hub - Jungle, the GameZone, and the Employee Experience Zone that appeal to both clients and employees. Learn more.

Job Summary

We are looking for a motivated and detail-oriented Data Analyst Fresher to join our team. The ideal candidate should have a basic understanding of data analysis, reporting, and visualization. This role is suitable for fresh graduates who are eager to build a career in data analytics.

Key Responsibilities
• Collect, clean, and organize data from various sources.
• Analyze datasets to identify trends and patterns.
• Prepare reports and dashboards for business insights.
• Assist in data validation and quality checks.
• Generate daily, weekly, and monthly reports.
• Work with team members to understand business requirements.
• Maintain accurate records and documentation.
• Support data-driven decision-making processes.

Required Skills
• Basic knowledge of Microsoft Excel (Formulas, Pivot Tables, Charts).
• Basic understanding of SQL.
• Knowledge of Power BI or Tableau is an added advantage.
• Basic understanding of data analysis and reporting.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
• Good communication and presentation skills.
• Attention to detail and willingness to learn.

Qualification
• B.E./B.Tech (CSE, IT, AIML), B.Sc. (Computer Science, Mathematics, Statistics), BCA, B.Com (Computer Applications), MCA, M.Sc. (Computer Science/Statistics), or any relevant degree.

Experience
• Freshers are welcome to apply.
• Internship or academic project experience in Data Analytics is an added advantage.

Salary & Benefits
• Attractive Salary
• ESI & PF Benefits
• Training and Mentorship
• Career Growth Opportunities

Job Type: Full-time

Pay: ₹12,000.00 - ₹15,000.00 per month

Benefits:
• Paid sick time
• Provident Fund

Work Location: In person
